title: data 20002015
wikitext:
[]: data 2000=2015             Quote Post by Deyno » Fri May 27, 2011 10:52 am End of Evangelion Images SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri May 27, 2011 12:03 pm I could be wrong, but these seem to be variables for a game or simulator. Perhaps this is the code for one of GAINAX's PC games?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri May 27, 2011 12:24 pm The comparison between the years 2000 and 2015 were to demonstrate that the data occurring at that time matched the data that was recorded fifteen years prior (Second Impact), simply meaning that Third Impact had begun.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri May 27, 2011 12:39 pm The code looks like some simple Windows scripting language with all whitespace removed in the first two images, and then properly fomatted in the last -- there are things that are clearly semi-colon separated, backslash punctuated search paths e.g. for midi files at various fidelities. The 2015 caption for "what is happening right now in the story" (in conjunction with the episode 26 eyecatch) is responsible for the bizarre notion that all the main action of EoE happens on 31-Dec-15 -- something I'm sure was not the intention.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri May 27, 2011 2:19 pm So the Bridge Bunnies were screwing around on the job when things got real? No wonder shit hit the fan so hard.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri May 27, 2011 11:11 pm NAveryW wrote: I could be wrong, but these seem to be variables for a game or simulator. Perhaps this is the code for one of GAINAX's PC games?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat May 28, 2011 2:38 am And the secrets that Misato discovers earlier in the movie are the history of Gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat May 28, 2011 2:42 am Mr. Tines wrote: And the secrets that Misato discovers earlier in the movie are the history of Gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat May 28, 2011 10:15 am chaosakita wrote: Really? That's not surprising I guess. It is from some company statement in English or what? I would really like to see a screencap of this.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat May 28, 2011 12:11 pm Mr. Tines wrote: And the secrets that Misato discovers earlier in the movie are the history of Gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat May 28, 2011 4:57 pm I think it's actually Seele = Gainax. MIND BLOWN.

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Sun May 29, 2011 2:44 am ¬ ¬ digression!

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Mon May 30, 2011 1:38 pm directed by M.Night Shamalama dingdong

[]:             Quote Post by Zoop » Tue May 31, 2011 2:06 pm chaosakita wrote: For instance, in Fullmetal Alchemist, a large amount of the words in the books shown were from DnD manuals or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 23, 2014 11:40 am Fernando sent this to the IRC channel: http://moviecode.tumblr.com/post/81558412688/taken-from-the-anime-film-the-end-of-evangelion They identified it as source code from the game "Gearheads", but there's no actual source code or snippets given. No idea how they matched it to the game.

[]:             Quote Post by Fernando » Wed Apr 23, 2014 6:26 pm Ornette wrote: Fernando sent this to the IRC channel: http://moviecode.tumblr.com/post/81558412688/taken-from-the-anime-film-the-end-of-evangelion They identified it as source code from the game "Gearheads", but there's no actual source code or snippets given. No idea how they matched it to the game.

[]: Re: data 2000=2015             Quote Post by KMG » Fri Oct 04, 2024 10:55 am Sorry to necro an old thread, but I thought this might be helpful. I recently rewatched EoE and was also curious about the text mentioned here. So I searched for some of the files mentioned in the code (which turned out to be midi tracks) and found this: https://modland.antarctica.no/incoming/ ... Gearheads/ Someone has archived midi tracks from Gearheads, and at the very least, the names of the midi tracks match those in the text from EoE.

[]: Re: data 2000=2015             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sat Oct 19, 2024 7:06 pm Interesting, thanks for finding that out.

[]: Re: data 2000=2015             Quote Post by Javi2541997 » Sat Nov 02, 2024 10:53 am It was a pleasure to read this old thread. There are precious gems hidden at the bottom of this forum. It is priceless when they pop out randomly often.
